title: Decoder pipelined ranker architecture space
hypothesis: The measured r64/k1 unpipelined producer-ranker wrapper is too slow because it charges a wide combinational ranker-to-merge path. Splitting the 64-logit tile into parallel local rankers, registering local winners, and registering the global winner before the merge FIFO should expose a more realistic architecture space with lower critical path at modest area/power cost.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Which segmented r64/k1 ranker parallelism point gives the best first timing/PPA tradeoff after adding register cuts?
include: local ranker widths 8, 16, and 32 over the same 64-logit tile
two register cuts: after local rank winners and before candidate merge FIFO
generated RTL simulation against the deterministic ready-valid reference
comparison against the unpipelined r64/k1 physical wrapper result

expected_benefit: replaces the optimistic wide-ranker assumption with measured pipelined variants
separates local-ranker parallelism from global-selection timing
keeps stream equivalence and II=1 ready-valid semantics explicit before scaling to r128
risks: the top-level wrapper still exposes a 64-logit tile and may retain standalone pin-pressure artifacts
two register cuts add latency even if throughput remains II=1
a single floorplan point may need follow-up tuning for the best segmented variant

required_evaluations.objective: Measure r64/k1 segmented ranker wrappers with local rank widths 8, 16, and 32 plus register cuts after local rank and before merge FIFO.

expected_result.reason: If pipelined segmentation reduces the critical path versus the 32 ns unpipelined wrapper, use the best local width as the r64 anchor before r128 or producer-coupled scaling. If not, revisit ranker algorithm or stream granularity.
